The application to amend the Zoning By-law proposes two new buildings for the property at 333-351 King Street East & 200 Front Street East. The proposed development includes a 37-storey residential building (140 metres tall) on the west side of the site and a 28-storey office building (127 metres tall) in the centre of the site. The footprint of the new office building would replace some of the existing retail uses fronting on Front Street East. The existing Globe and Mail Centre, Coca-Cola/George Brown building and existing buildings fronting on King Street East would remain in place. The proposal also includes two privately owned publicly-accessible spaces fronting onto Front Street East, and a child care facility with outdoor space on the second floor of the proposed office building.

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes the development of a 7-storey residential building. The building will provide a total of 40 residential units, being a mix of 2-bedroom and 3-bedroom.  A paved access from Finch Ave East will be provided, leading to 1 level of at-grade parking, providing a total of 22 parking spaces; as well as 29 bicycle parking spaces. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 3806.60 square metres and a FSI of 3.31. Additionally, the proposed development provides 88.8 square metres of indoor amenity space and  101 square metres of rooftop outdoor amenity space.

This site plan application proposes the demolition of the existing Petro Canada gasstation located at 2900 Finch Ave E. The redevelopment includes theconstruction of new gas pumps and canopy, a new convenience store/quick-servicerestaurant with an associated drive-through facility, and comprehensivelandscaping throughout the property. The existing car wash facilities will remain a partof the development.

Part Lot Control Application for Lots 8 and 9 within the larger site. These two lots were initially submitted in another Part Lot Control Application ( File: 25 157257 ESC 25 PL) which is currently under review. A seperate application was required for Lots 8 and 9, so that a separate Part Lot Control By-law can be passed by Council unencumbered to the restrictive conditions that are to be imposed on the Draft Plan of Common Elements Condominium/Part Lot Control Applications with respect to the units being built out on Blocks 11 and 12 on the same subdivision plan (City Files: 25 157275 ESC 25 CD/ 25 157257 ESC 25 PL ).

Proposal includes three buildings. Building "A": A third floor addition to the existing 2-storey building. Building "B": The existing three-storey building (1,387 m2) to remain with interior modifications and renovations only. The first floor will continue to house a synagogue and administrative functions, while the second and third floors will be converted into dormitory accommodations. Building "C": A new three-storey addition that will be constructed and integrated with Building A. The basement level will feature a cafeteria, while the first and second floors will be dedicated to classrooms and offices. The third floor will connect with the newly constructed third floor of Building A to form a dormitory space and additional classrooms.

Combined Official Plan & Zoning By-law Amendment for a 2 tower mixed-use building (45 and 49 storeys) atop the historic Dominion Public Building. There are no proposed changes to the built form permitted by previously-approved OPA 507 and Site Specific By-law 1251-2022. There will be a reduction to the amount of required non-residential gross floor area ('GFA") and an increase in the permitted residential GFA. The application also proposes to modify the north elevation of the Dominion Public Building to support the re-use of the building's ground floor as retail space. A new 500 square metre community space is proposed within the building.

Proposed Official Plan Amendment and Zoning By-law Amendment application to permit the development of the subject site with a 2- to 3-storey Performance Centre for the Toronto Tempo Women's National Basketball Association (WNBA) team.

Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment application to redevelop the southeast and southwest corners of the site at 310-330 Front Street West with two new mixed-use buildings. The proposed 70-storey tower to be located at the southeast corner (310 Front St. W.) would contain 929 residential units, 440 square metres of retail at grade and a total GFA of 58,849 square metres. The proposed 65-storey tower to be located at the southwest corner (330 Front St. W.) would contain 853 residential units, 303 square metres of retail at grade and a total GFA of 54,100 square metres. The existing enclosed atrium currently connecting the existing three buildings on the site is proposed to be replaced with a partially covered public plaza at the centre of the site. The site currently contains 399 parking spaces located in a 3-storey underground garage. These parking spaces will be retained but some will be reallocated for residential uses. An existing ramp and surface parking at the northwest corner of the site will be removed. A new, two-way vehicle ramp will be provided off Blue Jays Way at 330 Front St W. This application replaces the previously approved development at 310 Front St W. (File No. 21-146121 STE 10 OZ).

Original proposal for a 4-storey, 70 unit residential development with 68 underground parking spaces. Application has been appealed to the OMB and has been revised in accordance with the following: a reduction in the number of units from 70 to 42 and a shift from traditional apartment house dwelling units to back-to-back stacked townhouse units; a decrease in the proposed building coverage; the addition of a communal outdoor landscaped amenity area, doubling the overall amount of soft landscaping on the site; a shortened driveway due to a proposed car lift in replacement of the previously proposed car ramp; an increase in setbacks along the Finch and Elmview Avenue frontages and rear yard; removal of the previously proposed rooftop mechanical units; better integration of waste management facilities.

Site Plan Approval application to facilitate the development of Block 4 of the former LCBO lands, part of the comprehensively planned Lower Yonge Precinct.  Blocks 1 and 2 of the LCBO lands are already under development, and Block 3 has been conveyed as a City Park. The proposal is comprised of 3 towers having heights of 70, 78, and 85 storeys atop individual mixed-use podiums.  The north building includes a 990 m² daycare and space for a 5,613 m² elementary public school, both to be located in the existing heritage building on site.  A total of 1,291 m² of non-residential floor area is proposed as well as 173,713 m² of residential GFA, resulting in 2,606 residential units including 178 affordable housing units.

Official Plan and Zoning By-Law Amendment application that proposes to redesignate the subject site from "Regeneration Areas"  to "Mixed Use Areas", and permit two mixed-use buildings with heights of 44 storeys (Building A), and 47 storeys (Building B),  inclusive of independent 6-storey podiums for each building.  The existing one-storey commercial building on the subject site is not proposed to be retained.  The proposed buildings would have a total gross-floor-area (GFA) of approximately 75,165 square metres, comprised of 68,760 square metres of residential GFA and 6,405 square metres of non-residential GFA. The residential component proposes 1,136 dwelling units. The proposed non-residential component includes a mix of childcare space, a grocery store, and retail and service commercial space. One level of underground parking is proposed that would be shared between Building A and Building B with 220 vehicle parking spaces. The application also proposes 993 square metres of parkland dedication, located at the southwest corner of the subject site. Independent of the proposed parkland dedication area is a proposed 10-metre natural buffer area along the entirety of the west lot line, adjacent to Duncanwoods Greenbelt.

Application to create new and reconfigured public streets, public parks and 8 new Toronto Community Housing lots, to permit new and replacement residential development in Phase 2 of TCHC Lawrence Heights revitalization.

OPA & Rezoning Application proposes Phase 2 of the TCHC Lawrence Heights revitilization, including new and reconfigured public streets, public parks and community uses, and residential development ranging from 6 to 29 storeys in height. Phase 2 includes the replacement of 213 Phase 2 social housing units, 64 replacement units from Phase 4, and the creation of 180 new TCHC affordable units and 961 market units (total of 1,748 dewlling units).

Proposed redevelopment of the vacant, City-owned land at 4610 Finch Avenue East (Subject Site), for the purposes of a Long-Term Care Home as well as space for an Adult Day Program, EMS outpost station, and Toronto Paramedic Services administration (Proposed Development).
Proposed addition to existing manufacturing facility.

Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment application for 52-storey mixed-use building that would integrate the existing designated heritage building into the base of the tower and would contain approximately 546 square metres of retail space on the ground floor and 599 rental dwelling units above (including 16 affordable units).

Original proposal for a 4-storey, 70 unit residential development with 68 underground parking spaces. Application has been appealed to the OMB and has been revised in accordance with the following: a reduction in the number of units from 70 to 42 and a shift from traditional apartment house dwelling units to back-to-back stacked townhouse units; a decrease in the proposed building coverage; the addition of a communal outdoor landscaped amenity area, doubling the overall amount of soft landscaping on the site; a shortened driveway due to a proposed car lift in replacement of the previously proposed car ramp; an increase in setbacks along the Finch and Elmview Avenue frontages and rear yard; removal of the previously proposed rooftop mechanical units; better integration of waste management facilities.
Draft Plan of Subdivision application to create 3 blocks. This application is related to Zoning By-law Amendment Application No. 24 105045 ECS 22 OZ.

Zoning By-law Amendment for 7 blocks (A-F) of 3 connected buildings at 4 storeys each. There will be 122 units in the buildings will range from 1-4 bedrooms. The development has an FSI of 1.92 and a gross floor area of 11,645.69m2.

Site Plan Control Application to facilitate Phase 1 of the proposed redevelopment of the Jane Finch Mall. Phase 1 incorporates the lands fronting on Finch Avenue West. Six new buildings are proposed, with tower heights ranging from from 50 to 27 storeys. In total, Phase 1 proposes to deliver 2,730 new residential units. A total of 5,667.6 square metres of non-residential gross floor area is proposed, including 4,703 square metres of retail, approximately 465 square metres of indoor community space, and a 500-square metre day care centre.During Phase 1, most of the Jane Finch Mall is proposed to be retained, with approximately 2,500 square metres proposed to be demolished.Application relates to Official Plan and Zoning By-law amendment application file number 23 184369 WET 07 OZ.

Proposed two residential towers with heights of 36 and 31storeys, connected by a 5-storey non-residential podium. The proposed development will retain the existing 4-storey medical office building on the site, with approximately 8,000 square metres of gross floor area. The proposal would have a total of 10,883 square metres of new office space (in addition to the 8,000 square metres of existing office space), 549 square metres of retail space, and 38,771 square metres of residential space with 598 residential units,  overall density of floor space index (FSI)  5.96. A total of 544 parking spaces are proposed located within an underground garage.
